title: Learning-Augmented Adaptive Guidance For Target Interception
abstract: This paper presents a hybrid learning-augmented guidance strategy in which Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) is employed to improve the Proportional-Navigation (PN) guidance for target interception involving a maneuvering target. Unlike classical PN guidance, where the navigation constant N is fixed, our approach learns N as a function of the states of the system, including the line-of-sight parameters and relative dynamics. This adaptive cooperative guidance enables the pursuers to respond dynamically to the evader's maneuvers, optimizing interception performance. We formulate the problem as a Markov Decision Process (MDP) and train an RL agent using the Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO) algorithm. The agent learns to control the pursuer's lateral acceleration by varying N based on real-time observations. Experimental results demonstrate the superiority of the proposed hybrid learning-augmented guidance strategy over classical PN with fixed N=3 in scenarios with a maneuvering target. In addition, we also discuss the role of reward shaping in enhancing convergence and interception performance.

fields.title: Learning-Augmented Adaptive Guidance For Target Interception
fields.author: Sharma, Rajnikant and Sinha, Abhinav and Weintraub, Isaac E
